**Duplicate-staging guard checks local `status`, not VPS state** The guard condition on line 307 ΓÇö `config.status === 'staged'` ΓÇö reads from the **local** manifest being deployed, while `previousReleaseId` comes from the VPS config. In the normal re-deploy scenario a developer would commit `status: "scheduled"` (the pre-staging value), which causes the condition to be `false` even when the same `releaseId` is already staged on the VPS. The guard therefore never fires for the case it is designed to prevent, silently allowing a re-stage of an already-staged release without the `force` flag.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/lib/production-release.mjs Line: 303-312 Comment: **Duplicate-staging guard checks local `status`, not VPS state** The guard condition on line 307 ΓÇö `config.status === 'staged'` ΓÇö reads from the **local** manifest being deployed, while `previousReleaseId` comes from the VPS config. In the normal re-deploy scenario a developer would commit `status: "scheduled"` (the pre-staging value), which causes the condition to be `false` even when the same `releaseId` is already staged on the VPS. The guard therefore never fires for the case it is designed to prevent, silently allowing a re-stage of an already-staged release without the `force` flag. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,104 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Promote a staged production release (symlink swap + PM2 restart).
+# Run on the VPS as root. Requires /opt/gfc-analytics/config/production-release.json
+set -euo pipefail
+
+GFC_WEB_ROOT="${GFC_WEB_ROOT:-/var/www/gfc}"
+GFC_STAGING_WEB_ROOT="${GFC_STAGING_WEB_ROOT:-/var/www/gfc-staging}"
+ANALYTICS_ROOT="${ANALYTICS_ROOT:-/opt/gfc-analytics}"
+CONFIG_PATH="${CONFIG_PATH:-$ANALYTICS_ROOT/config/production-release.json}"
+PM2_APP="${PM2_APP:-gfc-analytics}"
+FORCE="${1:-}"
+
+if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_PATH" ]; then
+  echo "Missing config: $CONFIG_PATH" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+read_manifest() {
+  node -e "
+    const fs = require('fs');
+    const config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('$CONFIG_PATH', 'utf8'));
+    console.log([config.version, config.releaseId, config.rolloutAt || '', config.status || ''].join('\t'));
+  "
+}
+
+IFS=$'\t' read -r VERSION RELEASE_ID ROLLOUT_AT STATUS < <(read_manifest)
+
+if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
+  echo "Invalid manifest: missing version" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+RELEASE_DIR=$(node -e "console.log('$VERSION'.replace(/[^0-9A-Za-z.-]+/g, '_'))")
+
+WEB_RELEASE="$GFC_WEB_ROOT/releases/$RELEASE_DIR"
+ANALYTICS_RELEASE="$ANALYTICS_ROOT/releases/$RELEASE_DIR"
+
+if [ ! -d "$WEB_RELEASE" ]; then
+  echo "Staged web release not found: $WEB_RELEASE" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+if [ ! -d "$ANALYTICS_RELEASE" ]; then
+  echo "Staged analytics release not found: $ANALYTICS_RELEASE" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+if [ "$STATUS" = "live" ] && [ "$FORCE" != "--force" ]; then
+  echo "Release $RELEASE_ID is already live."
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+if [ -n "$ROLLOUT_AT" ] && [ "$FORCE" != "--force" ]; then
+  node -e "
+    const rolloutAt = Date.parse('$ROLLOUT_AT');
+    if (Number.isNaN(rolloutAt)) {
+      console.error('Invalid rolloutAt in manifest');
+      process.exit(1);
+    }
+    if (Date.now() < rolloutAt) {
+      console.error('rolloutAt has not passed yet:', '$ROLLOUT_AT');
+      process.exit(1);
+    }
+  "
+fi
+
+echo "Promoting release $VERSION ($RELEASE_ID) ..."
+
+ln -sfn "$WEB_RELEASE" "$GFC_WEB_ROOT/current"
+mkdir -p "$GFC_STAGING_WEB_ROOT"
+ln -sfn "$WEB_RELEASE" "$GFC_STAGING_WEB_ROOT/current"
+
+ln -sfn "$ANALYTICS_RELEASE" "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/current"
+
+cd "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/current"
+npm install --omit=dev --quiet
+mkdir -p "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/logs"
+
+if [ -f "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/config/.env" ]; then
+  cp "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/config/.env" "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/current/.env"
+elif [ -f "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/.env" ]; then
+  cp "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/.env" "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/current/.env"
+fi
body:
**Production `.env` overwrites staging server's config on promotion** During staging, `$STAGING_ANALYTICS_ROOT/current` is symlinked to the same physical directory as `$ANALYTICS_ROOT/releases/$RELEASE_DIR`, so both servers share one `node_modules` and `.env` path. When promotion runs `cp "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/config/.env" "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/current/.env"` (line 80), it writes production credentials to the shared physical release directory ΓÇö overwriting whatever staging env was written there in the deploy step. If the `gfc-staging-analytics` PM2 process restarts after promotion (crash, server reboot, etc.), it will read the production `.env` and connect to production Firebase, Stripe, and other services. Consider writing staging's `.env` to an out-of-tree path (e.g. `$STAGING_ANALYTICS_ROOT/config/.env`) and passing it via `pm2 restart --update-env` or `NODE_ENV` overrides, rather than placing it inside the shared release directory.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/release/promote-release.sh Line: 79-83 Comment: **Production `.env` overwrites staging server's config on promotion** During staging, `$STAGING_ANALYTICS_ROOT/current` is symlinked to the same physical directory as `$ANALYTICS_ROOT/releases/$RELEASE_DIR`, so both servers share one `node_modules` and `.env` path. When promotion runs `cp "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/config/.env" "$ANALYTICS_ROOT/current/.env"` (line 80), it writes production credentials to the shared physical release directory ΓÇö overwriting whatever staging env was written there in the deploy step. If the `gfc-staging-analytics` PM2 process restarts after promotion (crash, server reboot, etc.), it will read the production `.env` and connect to production Firebase, Stripe, and other services. Consider writing staging's `.env` to an out-of-tree path (e.g. `$STAGING_ANALYTICS_ROOT/config/.env`) and passing it via `pm2 restart --update-env` or `NODE_ENV` overrides, rather than placing it inside the shared release directory. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,104 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Promote a staged production release (symlink swap + PM2 restart).
+# Run on the VPS as root. Requires /opt/gfc-analytics/config/production-release.json
+set -euo pipefail
+
+GFC_WEB_ROOT="${GFC_WEB_ROOT:-/var/www/gfc}"
+GFC_STAGING_WEB_ROOT="${GFC_STAGING_WEB_ROOT:-/var/www/gfc-staging}"
+ANALYTICS_ROOT="${ANALYTICS_ROOT:-/opt/gfc-analytics}"
+CONFIG_PATH="${CONFIG_PATH:-$ANALYTICS_ROOT/config/production-release.json}"
+PM2_APP="${PM2_APP:-gfc-analytics}"
+FORCE="${1:-}"
+
+if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_PATH" ]; then
+  echo "Missing config: $CONFIG_PATH" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+read_manifest() {
+  node -e "
+    const fs = require('fs');
+    const config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('$CONFIG_PATH', 'utf8'));
+    console.log([config.version, config.releaseId, config.rolloutAt || '', config.status || ''].join('\t'));
+  "
+}
+
+IFS=$'\t' read -r VERSION RELEASE_ID ROLLOUT_AT STATUS < <(read_manifest)
+
+if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
+  echo "Invalid manifest: missing version" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+RELEASE_DIR=$(node -e "console.log('$VERSION'.replace(/[^0-9A-Za-z.-]+/g, '_'))")
body:
**Shell variables interpolated unsafely into `node -e` scripts** `$CONFIG_PATH`, `$VERSION`, and `$ROLLOUT_AT` are expanded directly inside double-quoted `node -e "..."` strings (lines 21, 33, 55, 61, 98). If any of these values contain a single quote, the embedded JS will have a syntax error and the script will abort without clear diagnostics. A sufficiently crafted manifest value could also inject arbitrary JavaScript code. Prefer reading the config entirely inside Node.js (e.g. spawning a separate `.mjs` file or passing the path as a `process.argv` argument) rather than embedding shell variables in inline JS strings.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: server/release/promote-release.sh Line: 18-33 Comment: **Shell variables interpolated unsafely into `node -e` scripts** `$CONFIG_PATH`, `$VERSION`, and `$ROLLOUT_AT` are expanded directly inside double-quoted `node -e "..."` strings (lines 21, 33, 55, 61, 98). If any of these values contain a single quote, the embedded JS will have a syntax error and the script will abort without clear diagnostics. A sufficiently crafted manifest value could also inject arbitrary JavaScript code. Prefer reading the config entirely inside Node.js (e.g. spawning a separate `.mjs` file or passing the path as a `process.argv` argument) rather than embedding shell variables in inline JS strings. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
